skill.name: ask-user
skill.description: Guides assistants to ask focused user questions, present clear options, and decide when clarification is necessary.
skill.summary: Ask precise user questions only when a decision or clarification is needed.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, JWT text, and decision guidance as risky patterns. Review found these are documentation examples only, with no executable code, prompt injection, system reconnaissance, or weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Code Fence Marked as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static matches are Markdown code fences and an example AskUserQuestion call. They do not execute Ruby, shell, or external commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context shows fenced documentation examples, not executable backtick syntax. No command names, interpolation, or runtime path exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Text References Marked as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain metadata and option labels, including JWT and Sessions. No hash function, cipher, or cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text only discusses authentication options in a sample question. I found no weak algorithm call or cryptographic code at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Guidance Text Marked as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line advises asking when multiple valid approaches exist. It does not enumerate host data, inspect systems, or collect environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain process guidance under an ask-versus-decide section. No reconnaissance command, file access, or system query appears nearby.

content.value_statement: Assistants often lose time by asking broad questions or guessing important preferences. This skill gives a clear pattern for asking focused questions with useful options.
actual_capabilities: Guides assistants to ask specific clarification questions.
Shows how to present options with short trade-off descriptions.
Encourages batching related questions to reduce interruptions.
Defines when the assistant should ask and when it should decide.
Provides patterns for clarification, confirmation, decision, and scope questions.
limitations: Does not execute commands or automate external services.
Requires an environment that supports user question tools.
Does not validate whether user answers are technically correct.
Provides general workflow guidance, not domain-specific policy advice.

use_cases.title: Clarify Product Requirements
use_cases.description: Ask concise questions when a feature request has unclear scope, competing priorities, or missing acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Resolve Engineering Trade-Offs
use_cases.description: Present implementation choices with practical trade-offs before committing to architecture, authentication, storage, or deployment decisions.

use_cases.title: Reduce Support Backtracking
use_cases.description: Confirm goals and constraints before preparing troubleshooting steps, configuration changes, or customer-facing responses.

prompt_templates.title: Ask a Focused Question
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ask-user pattern. Ask one focused question with two or three clear options before continuing.

prompt_templates.title: Confirm Scope Before Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ask-user pattern to confirm the requested scope. Explain what decision depends on the answer.

prompt_templates.title: Present Trade-Offs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ask-user pattern to present the main options, describe trade-offs, and recommend one option with a short reason.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Related Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ask-user pattern to batch related decisions. Ask only questions that materially affect the next implementation step.

output_examples.input: The user asks for authentication but does not specify a method.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks whether to use JWT or sessions, explains the trade-offs, and states which choice it recommends.

output_examples.input: The user requests a faster dashboard without defining fast.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks whether the goal is a specific response time, faster loading than today, or better perceived performance.

output_examples.input: The user asks for several related setup decisions.
output_examples.output: The assistant groups the decisions into a short set of questions so the user can answer once.

best_practices: Ask only when the answer changes the next action.
Offer concrete options and explain the trade-offs briefly.
Batch related questions when they share the same decision context.
anti_patterns: Do not ask broad questions when a specific decision is needed.
Do not interrupt for choices that have a clear best practice.
Do not hide the reason for a question when trade-offs matter.

faq.question: What does this skill help an assistant do?
faq.answer: It helps the assistant ask clear questions when user input is needed for a decision or clarification.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It contains guidance and examples only. It does not execute shell commands or external tools.

faq.question: When should the assistant ask the user?
faq.answer: The assistant should ask when preferences, trade-offs, unclear scope, or wasted work risk affect the next step.

faq.question: When should the assistant decide without asking?
faq.answer: The assistant should decide when a clear best practice exists, the choice is low impact, or the change is reversible.

faq.question: Can this skill help with multiple questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends batching related questions so users can answer efficiently.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
